prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens with a dark, low-contrast shot of a man seated behind a desk or table. He appears to be wearing a dark jacket with a light shirt, possibly formal clothing. Behind him are vertically striped curtains. On the desk there are indistinct objects, including papers or fabric to the right and a dark rectangular object to the left.

The image then shifts to a television or monitor screen. The screen shows layered, semi-transparent imagery: a face or figure, colored shapes, and faint text or graphics. The surrounding frame of the television is visible, suggesting the viewer is looking at an image displayed on a set rather than directly at a scene.

Another monitor image follows, now mostly monochrome. It shows a silhouetted scene with figures or objects moving across a horizontal landscape-like background. Faint greenish text or display graphics are superimposed over the picture, but the words are not clearly readable.

The commercial cuts to an extreme close-up of a circular speaker cone or similar audio component. The round diaphragm and central dust cap are prominent, with light reflecting off the surface.

Next, a television/monitor displays a stage-like image with dark figures against a gray background. Across the screen, a bold horizontal black strip carries the large white word “MITSUBISHI.” The brand name dominates the frame.

The following shot returns to layered, reflective imagery. A person or figure appears partially visible through or overlaid with glass-like panels and window frames. The scene is difficult to parse because of reflections and superimposition, but it suggests a display environment with screens and shiny surfaces.

Later, the frame shows a home-electronics arrangement: a large speaker on the left, a television/monitor in the center, and a dark stereo or component stack on the right. The center screen displays a man wearing dark sunglasses and a suit, turned partly toward the camera. The image on the screen overlaps visually with reflections or a translucent blue-tinted overlay.

A similar setup remains visible in the next shot, slightly wider or shifted. The man in sunglasses is still shown on the central screen. The surrounding room includes a brick or tiled wall pattern and objects in the foreground, possibly a remote control or equipment on a surface. The left speaker and right-side audio components remain visible.

The commercial ends on a close-up of a television screen showing a black-and-white extreme close-up of a mouth with a thick mustache above the upper lip. The television frame is visible, and the “Mitsubishi” name or logo appears below the screen.
